compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 plan-review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification). Bright-lines fire here (external Jira REST integration; a contract-test + verified-fake testing architecture), so T1 applies.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 — what already passes):
- [T1a] Relevant prior art is genuinely considered before committing, not reinvented/repackaged. The Context section surveys Pact (consumer-driven contracts), vcrpy (semantic matchers), Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel (structure-not-value validation), Google SWE-at-Google "verified fakes" + its change-detector warning, and how real Jira clients test (`jira-python` live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox vs `jira-cli` on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira THROUGH production serialization). It converges on a deliberately right-sized choice and explicitly rejects the heavier options (full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact) as "overkill for a single team."
- [T1b] The chosen pattern's novelty is justified vs established approaches (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three). The plan adopts an established pattern rather than inventing one: story C (59ef) names the SWE-at-Google "verified fake" pattern directly (fake carries contract tests run against BOTH real and fake impl), and the fixtures-through-production-serialization approach is explicitly modeled on `jira-cli`. Change-detector bloat is called out and guarded against (SC5; story B's documented anti-change-detector guardrail). This is adoption of a well-trodden pattern, not a novel architecture needing extra justification.
- [T1c] Capability assertions are largely resolved/grounded: the convergence is tied to named tools, and the verified-fake honesty mechanism (59ef AC2: fake fails the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client) is concretely specified.

ONE LIGHT FINDING (T1c — unverified capability assertion, deferred to an open question that isn't tracked):
- [ ] Resolve or explicitly defer the "Jira Cloud published OpenAPI covers the field" capability assertion: story C/59ef's Optional bullet proposes validating real responses "against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field (investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question)," but this epic body has no Open Questions section and the OpenAPI-completeness question is not recorded anywhere on the parent, so the assertion's prerequisite investigation has no tracked home.
  - criteria: T1
  - location: story C (59ef-ff3e-5aea-47b1) "What" → Optional OpenAPI bullet; epic f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 body (no Open Questions section present)
  - evidence: 59ef text "validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field (investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question)"; the referenced "epic open question" is not captured in the epic description (no Open Questions section), so the gating investigation is unanchored. NOTE: this is genuinely low-stakes — the bullet is marked Optional and self-flags the spec-completeness uncertainty, so T1 still PASSES overall; this note only asks that the deferral be made trackable rather than dangling.
  - suggested_fix: add a one-line Open Question to the epic (e.g. "Does Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI cover comment/issuelinks/parent field shapes well enough to validate against? If not, drop the optional OpenAPI leg") and have 59ef's Optional bullet reference it by name, OR drop the OpenAPI sentence from 59ef so no unverified-capability assertion remains.

Overall T1 verdict: PASS. The prior-art / anti-repackaging bar is well cleared; the single note above is a trackability tidy-up on one Optional, already-hedged assertion, not a roadblock.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay): PASS. No findings.

The bright-line fires (novel testing architecture + external-integration capability assertions), so the overlay applies. Evaluated against all three T1 sub-checks the plan PASSES; recording affirmations for later passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 sub-checks satisfied):

(a) Relevant prior art is considered before committing — STRONGLY satisfied. The Context section runs an explicit, named prior-art survey before choosing the approach: Pact (consumer-driven contracts), vcrpy (semantic matchers / layered redaction), Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel (structure-not-value validation), the Google SWE-book "verified fake" + its change-detector warning, and a concrete comparison of how real Jira clients test (jira-python's live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox vs jira-cli's on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira served through production serialization via an in-process mock). The chosen design is plainly derived from jira-cli's pattern + the SWE-book verified-fake prescription rather than reinvented. Not reinventing/repackaging.

(b) Novelty justified vs an established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three) — satisfied. The plan does NOT claim a novel architecture; it deliberately right-sizes a known pattern and explicitly REJECTS the heavier established options with a stated rationale: "Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team)," and "explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat." Story C (59ef) grounds the verified-fake choice directly in "Software Engineering at Google." The single-team scoping is the justification for not adopting Pact/live-sandbox — a sound established-vs-tailored tradeoff, not unjustified novelty.

(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved — satisfied. The library capabilities relied on are either already in-repo (the Jira REST enrichment surface — search_issues, get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map — and the existing external/live pytest marker, both confirmed in production use) or correctly gated. The ONE externally-new capability assertion — "validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field" (story 59ef "What") — is NOT left load-bearing: it is explicitly marked **Optional**, caveated "investigate spec completeness first," and deferred to a named **epic open question** (i.e. a spike). Per T1(c)/decisiveness, an unverified capability routed to a spike before commitment is the correct handling, so no finding is warranted; the only nudge (confirm-or-drop the OpenAPI leg after the spike) is already captured by the T3 reviewer on 59ef and need not be duplicated here.

Net: T1 is one of the better-handled criteria in this plan — a real prior-art survey, an explicit established-vs-tailored justification, and the lone novel external capability kept behind a spike. Clean PASS.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay): PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies):
- T3(a) technical_feasibility — The plan integrates Jira via the existing acli client surface; every named touchpoint is already in production use, so the integration is achievable as described with no capability gap. Verified in-repo:
  - `search_issues` (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py:370), `get_parent_map` (acli_graph.py:189), `get_comment_map` (acli_graph.py:289), `get_issuelinks_map` (acli_graph.py:391) — the four enrichment methods the FakeAcliClient (story A / fe57) mirrors all exist with real signatures.
  - The production fetcher merge seam the plan flows fixtures through exists: `fetch_snapshot` (fetcher.py:480).
- T3(b) named endpoints exist — MATCH. The plan's named endpoint `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` is the one production already calls (acli_graph.py:236, 337, 431); the code even documents that the older `/rest/api/3/search` is RETIRED/HTTP-410 (acli_graph.py:201-202) and the `/jql` endpoint is the live replacement — i.e., this is a verified, in-use integration, not a new assertion. Outbound REST paths the plan touches (`PUT /rest/api/3/issue/{key}`, `.../comment/{id}`, `GET .../issue/{key}?fields=issuelinks`) are likewise already present in acli.py / acli_graph.py.
- T3(c) auth/HTTPS preconditions — stated and pre-established. `JIRA_URL` / `JIRA_USER` / `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are the existing credential triad (JIRA_API_TOKEN env-only secret per rebar.toml:30, docs/jira-sync-setup.md, .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml), and an existing live external suite already round-trips against real Jira under live-gating (tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1). Story A's `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` is explicitly live-gated and reuses these same credentials/endpoints.
- T3(d) SPIKE routing — not required. No critical capability gap exists: the strategy is deliberately hermetic (fixtures captured from real Jira, served through an in-process fake), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly scoped OUT as overkill for a single team. No new live external dependency is introduced.

Per the T3 ANTI-FP rule (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable; verify before asserting a mismatch), there is no asserted-but-unverified external capability the plan depends on. Clean chunk for T3 — empty findings list is correct.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection). Grounded against the live codebase.

VERDICT: largely sound approach. ONE coaching finding (a mechanism/sequencing gap the plan under-specifies); the rest PASS.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this plan satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- G6 MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — PASS. The keystone mechanism (one producer↔consumer contract test fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures driven THROUGH the production code path) is mechanically realizable against current code. The production fetcher uses duck-typed enrichment — `if project_key and hasattr(client, "get_comment_map")` (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/fetcher.py:413), `hasattr(client, "get_issuelinks_map")` (fetcher.py:450) — so a no-massage FakeAcliClient flows through the real `fetch_snapshot` enrichment without a parallel path. The fetcher merges the raw nested `comment` key (fetcher.py:417) and the `issuelinks` list (fetcher.py:454) exactly as the bug-0ee6/3f04 root-cause analysis states.
- G6 FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — PASS. The approach solves the NAMED problem (a seam no test/probe spanned), not a proxy. The exact failure mode is corroborated in code: differs were unit-tested against hand-built dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits (e.g. tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_outbound_differ_comment_dedup.py:84 notes "the fetcher snapshot uses key 'comment' (not 'comments')"). The dual-applier drift is real — `apply_outbound.py` and `batch_dispatch.py` each carry a deliberately-duplicated `_index_existing_links` (batch_dispatch.py:42-63 comment: "local copy of apply_outbound._index_existing_links … the helper is duplicated"). The encoded-bug probe is real (scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1546 `pass_test "…inbound NOT implemented, as expected"`, :1547 `matrix_set … "NOT-SYNCED"`).
- G6 APPROACH SELECTION — PASS (alternatives generated and discarded). I generated two structurally-different alternatives: (a) JSON-schema validation on the PERSISTED snapshot artifact (data-layer) rather than a code-level schema module + round-trip test; (b) a single live-sandbox e2e (the jira-python pattern) instead of hermetic fixtures. The plan defensibly beats both: (a) would not exercise the producer→consumer SEAM through the production differ/applier code (the actual gap), only the at-rest shape; (b) is explicitly right-sized out ("Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team)") on blast-radius/CI-determinism grounds, matching the jira-cli hermetic-fixture pattern the plan cites. Chosen approach is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, testability, and simplicity. Alternatives discarded — not written into the plan.
- G6 POSITIVE RATIONALE — PASS. The Context's "Deep research … converges on a layered strategy right-sized for a single team … explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat" is an explicit positive rationale for WHY this approach fits (not merely a rejected-alternatives list).

FINDING (G6 — mechanism/sequencing under-specification at the D→E seam):
- [ ] Make story E's port-the-telemetry-and-canary step explicit, and tighten story D's parity gate to assert telemetry preservation — because the very capabilities E needs (`links_applied`/`comments_applied` counters AND the silent-no-op canary) already live in the typed-leaf path that story D DELETES, not in the surviving batch path.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: "Child stories" D (33d0-1e2c-e059-4181) + E (2359-ac1a-7c76-4f56); "Rollout & rollback" applier-collapse parity clause; Acceptance Criteria lines "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" and "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly"
  - evidence: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py already implements the telemetry — `links_applied += 1` (L357), the structured outcome `"links_applied": links_applied` / `"comments_applied"` / `"labels_applied"` (L387-392) — AND the silent-no-op guard `if not allowed and not labels_applied and not comments_applied and not links_applied and parent_key is None: logger.warning(... "no-op for %s ...")` (L372-385). The plan's Context says "production routes through the batch applier" and story D retires the typed-leaf path; the surviving batch path (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py) carries the per-mutation dispatchers but NOT these sub-op counters or the canary. So "collapse to the batch path" + "delete the retired path" would DELETE the only existing implementation of the E deliverables. The plan's parity gate is worded as behavioral ("the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did") — which a path can satisfy while still reporting `error=None` with no `links_applied` field, i.e. exactly the "sub-op success is silent" root cause #3 the epic names. The plan never states that E must PORT this logic into the batch path, nor that D's parity check must assert the structured-outcome telemetry survives.
  - suggested_fix: In story E (or a one-line note in story D), state explicitly that the sub-op counters and the `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary are to be MIGRATED from the retired `apply_outbound.py` into the surviving batch path (not re-derived from scratch), and extend story D's parity assertion from "applies every sub-op" to "applies every sub-op AND surfaces the same structured sub-op telemetry keys" so the collapse cannot silently regress the telemetry the very next story depends on.

NOTE: the recurring anti-change-detector-guardrail (AC "owned by story B") matter is a G3 coverage question, not a G6 approach finding — not raised here.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility — overlay): PASS / not-applicable. The plan does not adopt any new external API/CLI/service/library, nor assert a capability it has not used before. Every external surface it relies on is already in production use in this codebase and verified present: the acli transport + Jira REST endpoints (e.g. `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql`, `get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map`/`get_parent_map` in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py`, `get_issue_by_rest` in `acli.py`), the `links_applied`/`comments_applied` sub-op telemetry already emitted in `apply_outbound.py`/`apply_inbound.py`, and the existing live `jira-capability-probe.py`. Auth/HTTPS preconditions (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN) are already established by that probe and the live suite (`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`). The plan's strategy is deliberately hermetic (fixtures captured from real Jira, served through an in-process fake via the production fetch_snapshot path), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly out of scope — so it adds no new live external dependency. Under T3's ANTI-FP clause (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable), no findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness/anti-patterns/alternative-selection): PASS, no findings.

Grounded the mechanism claims against the live code:
- The producer↔consumer seam is real and exactly as described: fetcher writes `issuelinks` (fetcher.py:454) + nested `comment` shapes, while differs read those keys (inbound_differ.py:466, outbound_differ.py:969); the contract-test approach targets precisely this gap class.
- Story D's direction is consistent with the codebase: outbound mutations route through the legacy batch path `_apply_batch`/`update_one` (applier.py:474-490, batch_dispatch.py:388 "production routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf"), while the typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` is the path production does not use for outbound. Collapse-to-batch + retire-typed-leaf is sound; parity-before-delete (Rollout & rollback) handles the failure case.
- Story E's premise verified: the batch path returns the scalar `update_issue` result and dispatches links/comments/labels as side effects without surfacing counters (batch_dispatch.update_one + applier.py:700/785), whereas the typed leaf already builds `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` (apply_outbound.py:389-391). Surfacing these in the batch outcome + the `computed>0 && applied==0` canary directly detect the silent-no-op class. Warn-first→hard-fail rollout handles the failure-mode edge safely.

Approach selection: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) runtime schema-validation guards in fetcher/differ instead of a test-time contract; (b) VCR/record-replay fixtures only. The plan's hermetic-fixtures-through-the-production-path is at-least-as-good on blast-radius/testability/simplicity, and the plan explicitly weighs these in its Deep Research paragraph. Defensible → alternatives discarded (not added to plan). Positive rationale for the chosen approach is present and explicit ("one producer↔consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures... exercised through the PRODUCTION code path — explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat").

Affirmations: mechanism correctness, fitness-for-purpose (solves the named seam class, not a proxy), defensible approach selection, and present positive rationale all PASS.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 2 verification note (finding re: canary invariant `computed > 0 && applied == 0`): grounded against batch_dispatch.py:543-551 — outbound `update_one` skips a link `add` already present in either direction ("already present — no duplicate add"). On an idempotent re-sync, `computed > 0` (a link entry exists) while `applied == 0` though nothing failed. Inbound `links_applied` (apply_inbound.py:469-497) increments only on actual write, so the same idempotency class yields applied==0 with no failure. The plan's invariant as worded does not distinguish a failed no-op from a dedup-skipped no-op; worth disambiguating (e.g. count skips separately, or define `computed` as "ops attempted after dedup") before promoting to hard-fail.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — [T1] Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (overlay): PASS, no findings.

The plan crosses a bright-line (a novel-ish architectural pattern: a "producer↔consumer snapshot contract test fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the production code path"), so the T1 overlay fires — and the plan affirmatively satisfies all three checks rather than reinventing in a vacuum:

(a) Prior art is explicitly considered before committing. The Context's "Deep research" survey names and weighs the established alternatives — Pact consumer-driven contracts; vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis; Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation; Google SWE-book "verified fakes" + its change-detector warning; and how real Jira clients test (jira-python's live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox vs jira-cli's on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira through its production serialization). This is a genuine prior-art search, not a blind build.

(b) The novelty is justified vs the established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three). The plan does not repackage Pact or a live sandbox; it explicitly scopes "Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact … out of scope (overkill for a single team)" and converges on a layered strategy "right-sized for a single team." The Rule-of-Three is satisfied by three same-class production escapes (bugs 0ee6 / 3f04 / the expected-bug probe). Story B (8937) further mandates a documented ADR recording *why* one producer↔consumer contract test through the production path rather than Pact or a live sandbox — pinning the alternative-selection rationale as a committed artifact.

(c) No unverified capability assertions remain load-bearing. The chosen mechanism rests on well-trodden standard pieces — TypedDict + JSON Schema (jsonschema is already a core dep; verified per child fe57/8937 grounding) — and on the fetcher→differs→applier production seam that already exists in-tree (fetcher.fetch_snapshot + inbound/outbound differs + applier). The validation posture is anchored to an established practice ("Dredd/Gavel posture": assert structure/type, not exact values), so the contract test is calibrated against prior art, not novelty for its own sake. The cited library/tool capabilities are used as motivation/analogy, not as unverified "library supports X" load-bearing claims.

Affirmations (T1 checklist): relevant prior art considered before committing; novelty justified vs established approach (single-team right-sizing + Rule-of-Three + ADR capturing the trade-off); no unresolved unverified capability assertions. Clean — empty findings list for T1.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay): PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

The T3 overlay applies only when a plan integrates a NEW external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. Here every external surface the plan relies on is already in production use in this repo and verified present, so under T3's ANTI-FP rule (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable) this is not a load-bearing new-capability case.

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 sub-checks satisfied):
- T3(a) technical_feasibility — achievable as described, no capability gap. The four enrichment methods the FakeAcliClient mirrors all exist with real signatures: get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_comment_map, get_parent_map, search_issues; the production merge seam fixtures flow through (fetch_snapshot, fetcher.py:480) exists and already merges the nested `comment` key (fetcher.py:417) and `issuelinks` list (fetcher.py:454) exactly as the 0ee6/3f04 root-cause analysis states.
- T3(b) named endpoints exist — MATCH. The plan's named endpoint `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` is the live endpoint production already calls (acli_graph.py:426/431/435); code even documents the older `/rest/api/3/search` as retired/HTTP-410 with `/jql` as the replacement. Outbound REST paths the plan touches (`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?fields=issuelinks` at acli_graph.py:582, PUT issue/comment paths) are likewise already present. Verified, in-use — not a new assertion.
- T3(c) auth/HTTPS preconditions — stated and pre-established. JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN are the existing credential triad; an existing live external suite already round-trips against real Jira under live-gating (tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1), and scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py is explicitly live-gated (REBAR_CAPTURE_JIRA_FIXTURES=1) reusing those same credentials/endpoints, read-only.
- T3(d) SPIKE routing — not required. No critical capability gap: the strategy is deliberately hermetic (fixtures captured from real Jira, replayed through an in-process fake via the production fetch path), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly scoped OUT as overkill for a single team. No new live external dependency is introduced.

Clean chunk for T3 — empty findings list is correct.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Epic complete. All six child stories (A fe57, B 8937, C 59ef, D 33d0, E 2359, F 822a) are closed with signed completion-verifier PASS. Implementation shipped to main via squash-merged PR #43 (commit ea603e24). Closing-AC evidence: (1) single-source snapshot schema src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_snapshot_schema.py; (2) test validates fetcher.fetch_snapshot output vs schema (jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py); (3) differ reads validated by the same contract + round-trip; (4) producer->consumer round-trip through the production path on real captured fixtures (jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py + test_roundtrip.py); (5) outbound collapsed to one path — typed leaf delegates to batch_dispatch.update_one (apply_outbound.py 471->229 LOC); (6) retired parallel apply logic + its tests deleted; (7) sub-op telemetry links/comments/labels_applied surfaced on the batch outcome + RECON line (applier.py); (8) computed>0 && applied==0 canary fails loudly warn-first behind REBAR_RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP (test_silent_noop_canary.py); (9) hermetic bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip (test_roundtrip.py); (10) the inbound-comments NOT-SYNCED expected-bug probe guard de-encoded to fail-when-the-gap-reopens (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh); (11) anti-change-detector guardrail docs/adr/0004 committed + referenced by new tests; (12) full reconciler suite green (1043 passed).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

All 12 acceptance criteria and all 5 success criteria verified against the repository. Every requirement is demonstrably met.

### Acceptance Criteria

**AC1 — Single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module**
MET. `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_snapshot_schema.py` exists as a TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT covering all fields the differs read (`summary`, `description`, `status`, `priority`, `assignee`, `labels`, `issuetype`, `comment` (nested, bug 0ee6 seam), `issuelinks` (bug 3f04 seam), `parent`). It is referenced by `test_snapshot_contract.py`, `test_roundtrip.py`, and `tests/_jira_shape_contract.py`.

**AC2 — Test validates fetcher's snapshot output against schema**
MET. `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py` exists and drives `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output through schema validation, and is designed to FAIL on the pre-fix conditions (issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key).

**AC3 — Test validates each differ's snapshot reads against schema**
MET. Covered by the round-trip contract test in `test_snapshot_contract.py`, which drives both `inbound_differ` and `outbound_differ` reads through the production path against the schema-validated snapshot.

**AC4 — One round-trip contract test through the production path using real Jira fixtures**
MET. `test_snapshot_contract.py` + `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py` exercise `FakeAcliClient → fetcher.fetch_snapshot → inbound_differ + outbound_differ → applier` through the production path. Real captured fixtures exist at `tests/fixtures/jira/` (search.json, comment_map.json, issuelinks_map.json, parent_map.json, _meta.json).

**AC5 — Outbound application collapsed to a single production path**
MET. `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` (apply_outbound.py:125–161) is now a thin delegator to `_mutation_to_batch_dict` + `update_one`, mirroring `_apply_outbound_delete`. The ~240-line parallel implementation is gone.

**AC6 — Retired outbound applier path is deleted**
MET. The parallel apply logic is removed; old test files (`test_applier_outbound_update.py`, `test_applier_outbound_links.py`) are deleted. The leaf remains as a thin DISTINCT dispatch binding (required by the typed registry) but carries no parallel application logic.

**AC7 — Outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome**
MET. `applier.py:803–805` surfaces `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied` on the outcome dict from the `_subop` accumulator. `applier.py:865–869` surfaces all three on the `RECON: batch_outcome` line. `batch_dispatch.py:498/515/581/597` mints all as integer counters (post-dedup for links).

**AC8 — `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly (warn-first)**
MET. `applier.py:814–836` implements the silent-no-op canary (warn-first by default; hard-fail behind `REBAR_RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP=1`). `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_silent_noop_canary.py` asserts both flag states, the dedup-no-false-positive case, and the link-drop reproduction.

**AC9 — Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically**
MET. `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py` (280 lines) covers inbound (Jira→local) and outbound (local→Jira) for comments, links (both directions), and parent through the production code path with `pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration`.

**AC10 — Every probe assertion encoding a bug as expected converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes**
MET. `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1545–1550` — the previously inverted inbound-comments NOT-SYNCED guard is replaced with a positive assertion (`pass_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments (inbound comment synced — bug 0ee6 closed)"`) that fails on regression. The only remaining `NOT-SYNCED` occurrence is an explanatory comment at line 1535 documenting the de-encoding.

**AC11 — Documented anti-change-detector guardrail committed and referenced by new tests**
MET. `docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md` exists (ADR-style, status: Accepted). Referenced by `_snapshot_schema.py:22`, `test_snapshot_contract.py:18`, `test_roundtrip.py:22`, `test_outbound_update_combined.py:21`, and `tests/_jira_shape_contract.py:16`.

**AC12 — All six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green**
MET. All six stories closed with signed completion-verifier PASS: fe57 (hermetic fixtures + fake), 8937 (schema + round-trip), 59ef (verified fake), 33d0 (applier collapse), 2359 (sub-op telemetry + canary), 822a (bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding). Last epic comment records 1043 passed.

### Success Criteria

- **SC1** Producer/consumer cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing — MET (schema SSOT + joint round-trip contract).
- **SC2** Outbound sync exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path — MET (single thin-delegator leaf → update_one).
- **SC3** Silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green — MET (warn-first canary in applier.py + test_silent_noop_canary.py).
- **SC4** Bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected — MET (test_roundtrip.py + de-encoded probe).
- **SC5** New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic/type); suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added — MET (ADR guardrail enforces this; all new tests assert structure/type/round-trip outcomes, not blob equality or mock-call-counts).

**Verdict: PASS**

compiled_state.description:
## Context

Three production Jira-sync bugs escaped our tests this cycle, all the same CLASS — a gap at a *seam* that no test or probe spanned:

- **Inbound comments (bug 0ee6):** the inbound differ read snapshot key `comments`, but the fetcher writes the nested Jira-REST key `comment` — producer and consumer were unit-tested in isolation, so their shared shape was never jointly exercised.
- **Issue-links both directions (bug 3f04):** outbound link dispatch existed only in a *typed-leaf* applier that production does not use (production routes through the *batch* applier, which dropped links); inbound links were invisible because the snapshot never carried `issuelinks`.
- **A live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED** (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED`), so the defect could never be detected.

Root causes: (1) no producer↔consumer contract — differs are unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits; (2) two outbound applier paths drift (tests cover the dead typed leaf; production uses the batch path; capabilities were re-added one bug at a time — parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04); (3) sub-op success is silent (the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None`, never `links_applied`/`comments_applied`); (4) live probes are manual, not in CI, do not round-trip relationships, and can encode bugs as expected.

Deep research (Pact consumer-driven contracts; vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation; Google SWE-book "verified fakes" and its change-detector warning; and how real Jira clients test — `jira-python` uses a live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox, while `jira-cli` serves on-disk fixtures *captured from real Jira* THROUGH its production serialization via an in-process mock) converges on a layered strategy right-sized for a single team: **one producer↔consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the PRODUCTION code path** — explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat. Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team).

## Child stories

This epic decomposes into six stories (sized for one PR each):

- **A — Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client** (`fe57-7712-1e3f-45f4`): the foundation — fixtures captured from real Jira, served through the production path.
- **B — Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test** (`8937-eed3-c881-4ea1`): the keystone; also owns the documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact.
- **C — Verified fake: contract tests against BOTH real and fake client** (`59ef-ff3e-5aea-47b1`): keeps the fake honest.
- **D — Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one** (`33d0-1e2c-e059-4181`).
- **E — Sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary** (`2359-ac1a-7c76-4f56`): depends on D.
- **F — Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode "expected-bug" guards** (`822a-2bad-fe48-43e0`).

## Sequencing (MVP-first thin vertical slice)

The epic ships incrementally, not as a big-bang:

1. **A → B scoped FIRST to the known seam** — capture the inbound-comment (`comment`) and `issuelinks` shapes (bugs 0ee6/3f04) and land the single round-trip contract test on exactly those, before broadening the schema to all fields. This thin vertical slice de-risks the whole contract approach against the canonical failure.
2. **C** once the fake exists (keep it honest).
3. **D then E** — telemetry builds on the single consolidated applier.
4. **F** reuses A's fixtures for the hermetic round-trip and de-encodes the probe guards.

Each story is independently shippable behind its own PR.

## Rollout & rollback

- The **fail-loud sub-op invariant** (story E) lands **warn-first** (telemetry + `WARNING`) behind a flag/canary, and is promoted to hard-fail only after a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings; revert = drop back to warn (no other code change).
- The **applier collapse** (story D) verifies behavioral parity — the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — **before** the retired path is deleted.

## Success Criteria

- Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing.
- Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path.
- A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green.
- Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected.
- New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape.
- [ ] A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema.
- [ ] A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema.
- [ ] One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads.
- [ ] Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains).
- [ ] The retired outbound applier path is deleted.
- [ ] Outbound sub-op telemetry (`links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied`) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome.
- [ ] A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback.
- [ ] Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path.
- [ ] Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B).
- [ ] All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green.
compiled_state.title: Close the Jira-sync producer<->consumer & tested-vs-shipped test-coverage gap class
